results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: The debate was adjourned at the request of Ms Indranee Rajah, with proceedings scheduled to resume the following day. No new policy measures, legislative amendments, or operational constraints were introduced during this session. The House did not deliberate on any specific bills or government proposals before concluding the sitting.

results.title: Annual Data Trends on Contamination of Recyclables in Blue Bins from 2021 to 2026
results.summary: The Minister stated that the aggregated contamination rate for recycling bins and chutes under the National Recycling Programme has remained at approximately 40% since 2021, driven by incorrect placement of non-recyclables and food/liquid residues. The top contaminants by volume in 2025 were electrical and electronic waste, clothing, and composite plastics such as toys. Citizens are directed to deposit e-waste at designated collection points and donate usable clothes and toys rather than placing them in blue bins. Specific average rates for 2025 and detailed annual trend data were not provided in the response.

results.title: Annual Publication of SAF's Carbon Emissions to Improve Transparency of Climate Transition Plans and Outreach Efforts to Improve Public Understanding
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence does not plan to publish SAF carbon emissions separately, as data is already included in the annual GreenGov.SG report released by the Ministry of Sustainability and Environment since 2023. This report, accessible on the MSE website, aggregates emissions data while redacting sensitive information for security purposes. Consequently, the public can access SAF's climate transition metrics through this existing publication rather than a dedicated SAF-specific release.

results.title: Advertising Guidelines for Tuition and Private Education Services to Prevent Fear-based Marketing
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) is reviewing measures to discourage undesirable advertising practices by tuition and private education providers, noting that all advertisements are already subject to the Singapore Code of Advertising Practice, which prohibits capitalising on anxieties. MOE will engage parents, students, and educators in the coming months to reduce the "arms race" in education and attendant anxieties, while carefully studying options for sector-specific guidelines without rushing into additional measures. For egregious or outlandish claims on digital platforms, the public can file reports with the Advertising Standards Authority of Singapore. MOE continues to focus on the holistic development of students, building social-emotional competencies alongside academic results and 21st Century Competencies.

results.title: Assessment of Design Thinking Integration in Applied Learning Programme Development among Public School Students
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that Design Thinking is adopted as one of several problem-solving approaches within the Applied Learning Programme (ALP) to nurture 21st Century Competencies. Approximately one-third of public schools currently participate in external-partnered design challenges involving industry partners, higher learning institutions, and other organisations. The Ministry assesses the development of competencies like civic literacy and collaboration through internal studies, commissioned research, and international benchmarking rather than isolating specific programme impacts. Consequently, the qualitative effect of non-examinable programmes on solving real-world problems is evaluated holistically across diverse learning experiences including academic subjects and co-curricular activities.

results.title: Assessment of Effectiveness of 2019 Smart Bus Priority System Trial
results.summary: The 2019 Smart Bus Priority System trial, conducted for six months on routes 98 and 99, aimed to improve punctuality by adjusting traffic light timings. Although technically feasible, the trial did not substantially enhance bus punctuality. Consequently, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has no plans to scale up or deploy the system island-wide. LTA will continue exploring other methods to provide bus priority and improve travel times using better technology and data.

results.title: Assessment of Outcomes and Benefits of National Multimodal Large Language Model Programme towards Singapore's National AI Strategy 2.0
results.summary: The National Multimodal Large Language Model Programme (NMLP), launched in 2023, has developed two model families: Southeast Asian Languages in One Network (SEALION) and Multimodal Empathetic Reasoning and Learning in One Network (MERaLiON). These models enabled local scientists and engineers to build, fine-tune, and customise AI for regional contexts, while organisations like the Monetary Authority of Singapore and GoTo Group deployed applications serving Singapore and Southeast Asia. Collaboration with global partners such as Google Research and Microsoft Singapore further advanced model capabilities. The Minister confirmed these outcomes align with the objectives of Singapore's National AI Strategy 2.0.

results.title: Breakdown of Medical Social Workers across Public Healthcare and Community Care Settings from 2023 to 2025 and Average and Median Caseloads and Case Duration Managed Per Medical Social Worker
results.summary: As of 2023 and 2024, approximately 940 medical social workers were practising across public healthcare and community sectors, with 2025 figures pending. The Ministry does not track average or median caseloads or case durations, noting these vary by case complexity and setting. Manpower planning for the public healthcare sector is reviewed periodically to update requirements.

results.title: Building Ecosystem-level Awareness and Capability Pathways for SMEs to Join Space and Quantum Sectors
results.summary: The Government plans to build ecosystem-level pathways for SMEs in space and quantum sectors by leveraging existing strengths in aerospace, microelectronics, and telecommunications. Initiatives include the National Quantum Computing Hub's online learning platform for talent development and A*STAR's Technology for Enterprise Capability Upgrading programme, which supported Transcelestial in developing inter-satellite laser communications test stations. The Space Technology Development Programme (STDP) funded R&D projects benefiting companies like SpeQtral in satellite-based quantum key distribution technology. The STDP will transition to the National Space Agency of Singapore upon its establishment on 1 April 2026. The Singapore Space Community connects aerospace companies, startups, and agencies to foster collaborations.

results.title: Bus Stop Installation Adjacent to Blk 406 Fernvale Road Multi-Storey Car Park for Ease of Nearby Residents Commuting along Buangkok Drive
results.summary: Feasibility studies are underway for a potential pair of bus stops adjacent to Blk 406 Fernvale Road multi-storey car park to serve residents of Blocks 406A–406C and 407A–407B. Should the site be deemed feasible, design and construction for these stops will typically require six to nine months. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these timelines apply if the proposed locations along Buangkok Drive are approved. No specific completion date has been set pending the feasibility outcome.

results.title: Bus Stop Installation Adjacent to Blocks 174A to 174D Hougang Avenue 1 for Residents' Convenience Using Public Busses
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority is conducting site feasibility studies for new bus stops adjacent to Blocks 174A to 174D on Hougang Avenue 1. If deemed feasible, the design and construction phase will require approximately six to nine months. This initiative aims to improve public bus accessibility for residents in the vicinity, specifically targeting services like bus 146. The final timeline depends on the outcome of the ongoing feasibility assessment.

results.title: Capital Expenditure and Monthly Operating Costs for Value Meals @ South West Initiative
results.summary: The Value Meals @ South West initiative is a pilot programme partnering the South West Community Development Council (CDC) with Select Group to provide affordable meals via vending machines in heartland void decks for $3 or less. Funded entirely by Select Group's corporate social responsibility project, the CDC bears no upfront capital expenditure or monthly operating costs. As a pilot, the programme is not yet rolled out across all HDB towns, with CDCs instead building local networks with social and corporate partners to address specific district needs. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth clarified that this model represents one of various CDC-led initiatives delivering tangible benefits to residents.

results.title: Coffee Shop Operators Who Do Not Pass 5% Monthly Rental Rebate to Stallholders for Budget Meal Scheme
results.summary: Under the Budget Meal scheme, HDB eating house operators must pass the full 5% rental discount to stallholders providing budget meals. Operators are required to submit jointly signed letters of undertaking declaring how the discount is shared. To date, HDB has received no feedback indicating any operator failed to pass on the rental discount, and no corrective actions have been taken. The Minister confirmed that no instances of non-compliance have been reported since the scheme extended to all rented HDB coffee shops in 2023.

results.title: Construction of Staircase Linking Block 377B Hougang Rivercourt to HDB Car Park
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that HDB will construct a permanent staircase linking Block 377B Hougang Rivercourt to the adjacent car park to replace the existing temporary metal structure. Construction is scheduled to commence by the end of March 2026, with completion targeted for the end of July 2026. This timeline addresses resident safety and amenity concerns regarding the current temporary installation.

results.title: Data on Direction and Order Issuance for Platform Removal and to Restrict Access to Extremist Content in Past Three Years and Potential Review of Measures
results.summary: No directions or orders have been issued under the Online Safety (Miscellaneous Amendments) Act or the Online Criminal Harms Act in the past three years to restrict or remove content promoting radical, violent, or extremist acts. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs stated that such content is typically removed by platforms proactively or upon user reports, rendering legal compulsion unnecessary. However, authorities retain the preparedness to exercise these legal levers if required. The current measures are not under active review despite trends in online self-radicalisation among youths.

results.title: Data on Measles Cases by Residency Status and Sufficiency of Local Herd Immunity
results.summary: Between 1 January 2021 and 31 December 2025, 50 laboratory-confirmed measles cases were notified in Singapore, comprising three tourists and 47 residents, with six identified as breakthrough infections in vaccinated individuals. Vaccination coverage for resident children is approximately 93% at two years and 97% by seven years old, while seroprevalence data indicates 99% immunity among adult residents, exceeding the 95% herd immunity threshold required to protect vulnerable groups like infants under 12 months. Although Singapore remains vulnerable to imported cases due to high travel volumes and pockets of unvaccinated individuals, there is currently no evidence of sustained community transmission. High vaccination coverage, active surveillance, and prompt public health responses continue to safeguard against the re-establishment of endemic measles transmission.

results.title: Data on Mental Health History of Arrested Drug Abusers and Breakdown of First-time Drug Abusers with Prior Diagnosis of Mental Health Disorders since 2021
results.summary: The Central Narcotics Bureau does not collect mental health history data for arrested drug abusers. Instead, the Singapore Prison Service assesses all inmates, including first-time offenders, upon admission to prison or the Drug Rehabilitation Centre. Screening involves checking the National Electronic Health Record System and completing a questionnaire to identify pre-existing conditions. As of end-December 2024, approximately 10% of the total inmate population, encompassing those in the DRC, were diagnosed with a mental health condition.

results.title: Data on Singapore-owned Satellites and National Space Agency's Recruitment Plans
results.summary: Singapore has launched over 30 satellites, with the Government co-owning three Earth Observation satellites alongside ST Engineering for commercial and government use. The NeuSAR-2 constellation, comprising four Synthetic Aperture Radar satellites for commercial use, is scheduled to become fully operational in 2030. The National Space Agency of Singapore (NSAS) will initially draw officers from the Office for Space Technology and Industry before progressively ramping up its workforce over the next five years. Recruitment will span space technology, geospatial analytics, project management, industry development, and policy across the next three years.

results.title: Discrimination Complaints by Industry Sector Received by MOM and TAFEP from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that a breakdown of discrimination complaints by industry sector is available only for the second half of 2023 through 2025. Data for the years 2021 to 2022 is not provided in the response. The specific figures for these years are listed in Table 1 within the official reply. Consequently, the full historical trend requested from 2021 to 2025 cannot be fully assessed based on the current information.

results.title: Enforcement Actions against Non-compliant Public Waste Collectors and Collection Delays for Recycling at Pasir Ris-Bedok Sector Since 2024
results.summary: The National Environment Agency imposes financial penalties on Public Waste Collectors failing to meet licensing conditions, including scheduled refuse and recyclable collections. For the Pasir Ris-Bedok sector, collectors must collect recyclables thrice weekly for 660-litre bins and once weekly for 2,200-litre side loader bins. The Minister stated there have been no missed recyclable collections in this sector since 2024. Additionally, NEA collaborates with collectors to increase refuse and recyclable collection frequency during festive seasons.

results.title: Enforcement Against Individuals Who Dispose of Lit Cigarette Butts in Refuse Chutes and Adoption of Technology to Track Such Cases
results.summary: The National Environment Agency confirmed there have been no enforcement actions against individuals for disposing of lit cigarette butts in refuse chutes to date. The agency does not utilise surveillance analytics or thermal imaging for refuse chutes, as the Statutory Presumption under the Environmental Public Health Act 1987 applies only to high-rise littering from residential flats into public places, not refuse chute disposal. Consequently, no specific tracking technology or enhanced presumption measures are currently in place for this specific offence.

results.title: Expanding Usage of MediSave Funds for Purchase of Hearing Aids and Other Hearing Devices
results.summary: The Government will not expand MediSave usage for hearing aids but offers alternative subsidies via the Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund and Assistive Technology Fund, providing up to 90% means-tested support. Eligibility income caps for these schemes rose from $2,600 to $4,800 in January 2026. Additionally, MediSave and MediShield Life limits for severe cases like cochlear implants were enhanced in April 2025. Individuals requiring further assistance can approach the Agency for Integrated Care, SG Enable, or medical social workers at public hospitals. No one will be denied appropriate healthcare due to inability to pay.

results.title: Factors That Warrant Escalated Intervention to Tackle Monkey Intrusions in Estates
results.summary: NParks assesses monkey intrusion hotspots using a science- and community-based approach, evaluating public safety risks alongside population ecology and behavior. Escalated interventions include sterilization for long-term population control in high-conflict areas and removal of aggressive individuals to ensure safety. The agency collaborates with residents, town councils, and the Long-tailed Macaque Working Group to eliminate food sources and enforce bans on feeding wildlife. Standard deterrence measures are deployed first; if repeated damage persists, further estate-level actions are considered. Response benchmarks apply to repeated or aggressive incidents to determine the necessity for escalated intervention.

results.title: Fair Consideration Framework Watchlist Companies by Sector and Triple-Weak Sector Measures, Annually, from 2015 to 2025
results.summary: The Fair Consideration Framework (FCF) watchlist, introduced in 2016 to identify firms with high single-nationality concentrations and reliance on foreign PMETs, listed nearly 1,000 companies between 2016 and 2023. The majority belonged to the information and communications technology, professional services, and construction sectors. In 2023, the watchlist was discontinued as its criteria were integrated into the Complementarity Assessment Framework (COMPASS), eliminating the need for manual scrutiny of weak workforce profiles. Instead, the Ministry collaborates with the labour movement, including the National Trades Unions Congress and Tripartite Alliance, to support firms through Company Training Committees and workshops aimed at improving local hiring and workforce development.

results.title: Financial Incentives to Reward Producers who Design Products for Durability, Repairability and High Recyclability
results.summary: Singapore's Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) frameworks mandate upstream responsibility for end-of-life product recycling but do not currently include eco-modulated fees to incentivize durability or repairability, as overseas manufacturers are unlikely to adjust designs for the small domestic market. To address this, the Government provides targeted funding and partnership programmes, such as the 3R Fund, to support local businesses in redesigning products for circularity. The Minister stated that these initiatives aim to encourage eco-design and sustainable practices to reduce waste disposal, working alongside industry stakeholders to promote these outcomes.

results.title: Funding Framework for Distribution of Public AI Research Funds
results.summary: The Government commits over $1 billion for public AI research from 2025 to 2030, targeting fundamental research, applied research, and talent development. The Minister stated that precise allocations across these pillars will not be disclosed to allow flexible responses to emerging opportunities. Instead, funding distribution follows sound processes and expert judgement within existing governance frameworks for similar R&D programmes. The primary guiding principles are sustaining long-term investments, creating value in high-impact areas, and maintaining global competitiveness for Singapore's research capabilities and talent.

results.title: Guidelines and Parameters for Dancing and Other Such Activities in Public Spaces
results.summary: Dancing and similar informal activities in public spaces are generally permissible provided they do not obstruct the public or cause a nuisance. The Police will intervene solely when law and order concerns arise. The Government maintains that existing parameters balance public amenity with the vibrancy of public spaces without introducing new specific guidelines. No additional measures or timelines for regulation were stated during the session.

results.title: Impact Assessment of Investments into AI Manpower and Capability Development from 2022 to Date
results.summary: The Government assesses that the additional US$50 million investment in AI manpower and capability development has met its objectives since 2022. This funding supported the expansion of the AI Apprenticeship Programme (AIAP) and a new Singapore National Research Foundation (NRF) Fellowship for AI. Since 2018, the AIAP has developed over 400 skilled practitioners, with 85% of graduates securing AI engineering roles across the economy while 15% joined AI Singapore to scale the programme. Four NRF Fellowships have been awarded to anchor early-career researchers for independent work in Singapore, with progress being closely tracked by the NRF.

results.title: Individuals Who Exceed Eligibility Criteria for Silver Support Scheme Based on Household Income Per Capita
results.summary: The Silver Support Scheme uses a holistic assessment of lifetime wages, housing type, and monthly per capita household income (PCHI) to determine eligibility. The qualifying PCHI threshold increased from $1,800 to $2,300 in 2025, allowing more seniors, including some living with adult children, to qualify. For 2026, approximately one in ten ineligible seniors are excluded solely because their PCHI exceeds $2,300, often due to income from themselves or other household members. The government does not track the specific number of ineligible seniors residing with adult children. Seniors facing unique circumstances may write to the Central Provident Fund Board for a case-by-case review of their eligibility.

results.title: Instilling Awareness for Loot Boxes and Gambling-inducement Risks in Video Games Through CCE Lessons
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that Cyber Wellness lessons, a core component of the Character and Citizenship Education curriculum from primary to pre-university levels, teach students about gambling-like elements in online games. While these topics are mandatory, specific instruction on loot box mechanics is currently delivered in Secondary 5, with plans to introduce it earlier to lower secondary students. The curriculum emphasizes age-appropriate skills for resisting over-spending on in-game purchases and identifying gaming risks. The Ministry maintains that parents play a key role in monitoring online activities and controlling purchases, working alongside schools to protect children from online harms.

results.title: Lease Buyback Scheme Participation Statistics and Average Payouts by Household Size from 2009 to 2025
results.summary: As of end-2025, approximately 16,000 households have participated in the Lease Buyback Scheme (LBS) since its launch on 1 March 2009, comprising roughly 6,000 single-person and 7,000 two-person households. Eligible seniors monetize their flats by selling a portion of the remaining lease to HDB while retaining a lease sufficient for the youngest owner to reach age 95. Proceeds vary by flat type and market conditions, with about 90% of participants receiving between $100,000 and $300,000. Seniors topping up their CPF Retirement Account may additionally receive a cash bonus ranging from $7,500 to $30,000 based on their specific flat type and top-up amount.

results.title: Leveraging Artemis Accords and Related Events to Increase Awareness for Space Exploration
results.summary: Singapore is a signatory to the non-binding Artemis Accords, which promote peaceful and sustainable space use, but the Government is not involved in the upcoming Artemis II crewed lunar flyby mission as participation is optional for signatories. Beyond the Accords, Singapore has established bilateral space agreements with France, India, Luxembourg, Spain, Thailand, and the United Arab Emirates to support joint research, policy exchanges, and commercial interests in satellite communications and geospatial analytics. The Government recently hosted the Singapore Space Summit 2026 to strengthen partnerships across the space value chain among agencies, industry players, researchers, and investors. No specific budget was disclosed for leveraging the Artemis II mission, and no further outreach event numbers were provided beyond the summit.

results.title: MMR Vaccination Outreach Programme for Long-term Foreign Residents and Measures to Enhance Detection of Important Cases
results.summary: Since February 2019, all foreign-born children aged 12 and below applying for long-term immigration passes must be vaccinated or immune against measles. This requirement extends to healthcare workers, preschool staff, and migrant domestic workers in households with young children. Measles is a legally notifiable disease; medical practitioners and laboratories must notify the Ministry of Health within 24 hours of suspicion or diagnosis. The Communicable Diseases Agency investigates all notified cases, including imported ones, and implements public health actions. Given high vaccination coverage providing robust herd immunity, border surveillance measures are currently not required.

results.title: Measles Prevention Measures and Related Public Education Efforts
results.summary: Enhanced public health measures, including isolation, contact tracing, vaccination, and quarantine, were implemented to prevent measles spread as of 6 February 2026. These actions were amplified via government social media platforms with high public awareness. Under the National Adult Immunisation Schedule, all unvaccinated, partially vaccinated, or immunity-deficient adults are recommended to complete two doses of the MMR vaccine. Medical practitioners are directed to notify the Communicable Diseases Agency promptly regarding suspected cases. Adults uncertain of their status should consult healthcare providers for assessment and advice.

results.title: Measures to Prevent Speeding and Reckless Riding by Cyclists
results.summary: Cyclists must adhere to speed limits of 10 km/h on footpaths and 25 km/h on shared paths. Between 2023 and 2025, over 650 cyclists were penalised for disorderly riding without causing accidents. The Land Transport Authority and Singapore Police Force conduct joint enforcement operations, while LTA deploys speed detection radars, warning signage, and CCTVs at high-risk hotspots. Additionally, LTA patrols with the community to promote safe riding behaviours.

results.title: Mosques Operating on Temporary Occupation Licences and Long-term Tenure and Operation Plans for These Mosques
results.summary: Currently, seven mosques operate under Temporary Occupation Licences issued by the Singapore Land Authority. The Islamic Religious Council of Singapore (MUIS) plans to address long-term religious needs by working with relevant agencies to extend tenure or identify alternative arrangements, such as longer leases, where feasible. These actions are subject to overall land use plans. MUIS will continue to ensure adequate prayer spaces for the Muslim community while managing these operational constraints.

results.title: Processing Time for Applications to Convert Trailer Lots to Heavy Vehicle Lots
results.summary: Applications to convert licensed trailer lots to heavy vehicle (HV) lots are processed within three to five working days after complete document submission. Expedited processing is not mentioned. Typically, one licensed trailer lot yields one HV lot, contingent on the specific configuration of the parking lot and heavy vehicle park. The conversion depends on these physical constraints rather than a fixed yield ratio.

results.title: Production Cost of Minting Five-cent Coins in Past Five Years and Plans for Withdrawing from Circulation
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has not minted new five-cent coins in the past five years and currently has no plans to withdraw them from circulation. MAS estimates production costs remain below face value and cites continued public demand, noting that businesses actively price items using five-cent increments. Stopping issuance could force retailers to round up prices, potentially increasing consumer costs. MAS will continue monitoring usage patterns to assess the denomination's long-term relevance.

results.title: Profile of Occupants in HDB Public Rental Households
results.summary: As of end-2025, approximately 5,300 HDB public rental households (10% of total) house primary school-aged children, with 46% in Primary 1–2, 45% in Primary 3–4, and 42% in Primary 5–6. Median occupants per unit are two for 1-room flats and four for 2-room flats. The Government has not conducted studies linking overcrowding to poor learning outcomes but instead focuses on initiatives like ComLink+ to support lower-income families and improve their income. These efforts aim to help families eventually purchase their own flats rather than addressing overcrowding directly.

results.title: Progress Update and Completion Timeline of 15 Kilometres Forest-like Corridor Connecting East Coast Park and Changi Beach
results.summary: The East Coast Central Green Corridor, a 15km forest-like project connecting East Coast Park and Changi Beach announced in 2020, is currently in the planning and design stage. The Minister for National Development stated that the completion timeline will be announced when ready. No specific dates or progress metrics have been provided yet.

results.title: Reasons for Decline in Cancer Mortality despite Rising Diagnoses
results.summary: The Ministry attributes the 21% decline in cancer mortality since 2012 to advances in healthcare enabling earlier detection and better treatment under Singapore's universal access system. While prevention efforts targeting smoking, diet, and alcohol are emphasized, the Cancer Drug List (CDL) relies on unpublicized cost-effectiveness thresholds to maintain negotiating leverage, resulting in an average 30% price reduction for drugs. The subsidy framework ensures lower-income groups receive higher means-tested support, narrowing access gaps without restricting private insurance uptake for higher-income individuals. Consequently, the most clinically effective and cost-effective drugs remain available to all, with no specific timeline provided for threshold reviews or cost estimates for further gap reduction.

results.title: Regulatory Compliance Framework Involving Information on Genetic Disorder Diagnoses for Insurance Claims
results.summary: The Moratorium on Genetic Testing and Insurance prohibits insurers from requesting or using predictive genetic test results during underwriting to prevent risk pool fragmentation, except for very high-value products where additional information may be requested to ensure fairness. Medical information regarding manifested symptoms or formal diagnoses remains part of an individual's medical history and may be disclosed for underwriting purposes, provided the underlying genetic causes are not revealed. Once coverage is secured, the moratorium no longer applies, allowing policyholders to submit relevant medical history or diagnoses for claim assessment without secondary underwriting. The Ministry of Health will strengthen legislative protections for genetic information to provide greater public assurance moving forward.

results.title: Renewal Criteria of Migrant Domestic Workers' Work Permit and Measures to Expedite Renewal Applications
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) assesses renewal applications based on the employer's capacity to maintain the migrant domestic worker and the worker's age. Eligible employers receive renewal letters approximately two months before expiry, provided they secure a security bond and medical/personal accident insurance from a bank or insurer. Once these documents are submitted, the bank or insurer forwards information to MOM within three working days, after which the renewal application is approved immediately.

results.title: Rental Estates' Study Space Usage by Age Profile, Average Daily Usage Hours and Housing Type and Plan to Subsidise Study Furniture
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Government provides various public study spaces, including those in schools and community centres, but does not hold data on their utilisation rates by age, usage hours, or housing type. Regarding subsidies for space-saving furniture, the Minister did not confirm any plans to introduce such measures. For lower-income households requiring financial assistance, the Minister directed them to approach their Social Service Office or local charities. No specific timeline or eligibility criteria for furniture subsidies were provided in the response.

results.title: Restrictions or Quotas for LTVP Applications for Foreign Spouses and Breakdown of Approval and Rejection Rates by Nationality over Past Five Years
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that Long-Term Visit Pass applications for foreign spouses of Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are assessed based on marriage duration, children, sponsor support capacity, and adverse records. No nationality-based restrictions or quotas exist, though detailed criteria are not published to protect international relations and prevent system gaming. Furthermore, approval and rejection rates by nationality over the past five years are not released, as publishing such data could cause bilateral sensitivities and is not deemed in the national interest.

results.title: Review of $5,000 CPF Withdrawal Limit Quantum for Singaporeans at Age 55
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the $5,000 unconditional CPF withdrawal limit for Singaporeans at age 55, established in 1995, remains relevant and has not been increased since 2023. He explained that the Government views withdrawal rules holistically, noting that existing flexibilities allow members with savings above their cohort Full Retirement Sum to withdraw excess amounts, while property owners can set aside funds in a mixture of property and cash. Additionally, cohorts turning 65 since 2023 can withdraw up to 20% of their Retirement Account savings. The Minister emphasized that increasing withdrawal limits would trade off against lower monthly retirement payouts, particularly for those who have not met their Full Retirement Sum.

results.title: Review of SkillsFuture Framework and Costs of Courses to Better Support Upskilling and Reskilling
results.summary: SkillsFuture Singapore provides course fee subsidies up to 70% and an initial $500 credit at age 25, with mid-career Singaporeans aged 40+ receiving enhanced 90% subsidies and a $4,000 credit top-up. A training allowance covers up to 50% of average monthly income (capped at $3,000) to offset income loss during long-form training. The Minister confirmed that the framework is regularly reviewed to calibrate support for all Singaporeans, including specialised professionals facing AI disruption, ensuring career reboot opportunities remain accessible. While specific flexibility mechanisms for PMETs are under consideration, the overall structure aims to align support with the needs of those requiring substantive skills retraining.

results.title: Review of Smoking Prohibition Regulations at Casinos towards Enhanced Secondhand Smoke Protection for Employees
results.summary: Casinos in Singapore maintain separated smoking and non-smoking gaming areas under their house rules. Under the Workplace Safety and Health Act, operators must identify hazards and mitigate secondhand smoke exposure through measures like negative air pressure and improved exhaust airflow. Operators are expected to allow staff in smoking environments to use suitable Personal Protective Equipment, such as face masks, based on their own risk assessment and health protection needs. Specific provisions for older workers or strict separation mandates were not explicitly confirmed as new regulatory changes in this response.

results.title: Safeguards against Exploitative Excess Arrangements in Car Rental and Car-sharing Services
results.summary: The Government lacks data on accident repair costs for rental and car-sharing vehicles falling below insurance excess thresholds. It does not regulate contractual excess arrangements between operators and hirers, viewing them as commercial terms. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that introducing regulations would increase costs, likely passed to hirers via higher rental rates. Consequently, no safeguards against exploitative excess arrangements are currently implemented, and the Government will not intervene in these specific commercial agreements.

results.title: Shortening Timeframe for Identifying Students with Special Needs in Mainstream Schools to Expedite Support and Interventions
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding the current average timeframe for identifying special needs students in mainstream schools and the possibility of expediting this process has already been addressed via a written answer to Parliamentary Questions Nos 30 to 31 on 3 February 2026. No new policy measures, timelines, or operational changes were announced during this session. Readers seeking specific data on identification durations or future review schedules should refer to the cited written response for the complete details.

results.title: Statistics on Prolonged Vacancies for Hawker and/or Wet Market Stalls and Intervention Triggers for Stall Vacancies
results.summary: As of 31 December 2025, approximately 130 cooked food stalls (2%) and 280 market stalls (4%) across NEA-managed hawker centres have remained vacant for over six months. The National Environment Agency monitors these vacancies and assesses intervention needs on a case-by-case basis rather than applying a fixed trigger period. Interventions may include modifying allowable trade types where feasible to improve stall take-up. The Minister did not specify exact timelines for intervention or confirm if changes to stall use are currently being implemented for all prolonged vacancies.

results.title: Steps to Keep Water Affordable for Households
results.summary: Water pricing reflects scarcity, ensuring industrial users bearing higher consumption costs contribute more to supply expansion. The Ministry provides targeted support through U-save rebates for eligible lower- and middle-income HDB households to offset utility expenses. Additionally, the Mandatory Water Efficiency Labelling Scheme guides purchasing of water-efficient appliances to reduce consumption, while the $400 Climate Vouchers under the Climate Friendly Households Programme further offsets related purchase costs.

results.title: Streamlining SME Grant Applications and Approvals through Enterprise Singapore as a Single Point of Contact
results.summary: Enterprise Singapore partners with SME Centres to assign business advisors as single points of contact for coordinated support tailored to specific business requirements. For straightforward grant enquiries, SMEs can access eligibility criteria and step-by-step application guides via EnterpriseSG and GoBusiness websites as the first port of call. This approach allows SMEs to find resources conveniently without navigating multiple agencies for basic inquiries, while complex needs receive guided assistance through designated advisors.

results.title: Structural Unemployment Risks for Singaporean PMETs Aged 40s and 50s
results.summary: The Minister stated that structural unemployment risks for resident PMETs in their 40s and 50s remain low and stable, though rapid technological change necessitates regular upskilling. The Government has invested over $10 billion in five years to support this group through programmes like WSG's Mid-Career Pathways Programme, SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme, and Career Conversion Programmes, which offer salary support for reskilling into growth roles. Regarding supplementary questions, the Tripartite Workgroup for Senior Employment is exploring measures beyond SkillsFuture to help professionals pivot while still employed, addressing early skills obsolescence. While the NTUC facilitates lateral movements between adjacent sectors, the Minister noted that creating a tight labour market is not the primary tool; instead, the focus is on enhancing worker awareness of opportunities and providing comprehensive career employment services to enable pivots across sectors.

results.title: Studying Historical and Architectural Significance of Traditional Kampong-style Mosques for Conservation
results.summary: The National Heritage Board (NHB) acknowledges the heritage value of traditional kampong mosques, with Masjid Petempatan Melayu Sembawang already featured on the Sembawang Heritage Trail. NHB will collaborate with the Urban Redevelopment Authority to assess suitable buildings, including mosques, for preservation or conservation. This assessment considers heritage and architectural value, intended site use, community interests, and future development needs. The transcript does not confirm a specific timeline for conservation decisions or state that any particular mosque will be excluded from consideration.

results.title: Successful COE Bids by Households Already Owning at Least One Car
results.summary: Eighty-five percent of new Category A and B Certificates of Entitlement (COEs) were won by households already owning at least one car, with most being single-car households replacing existing vehicles. Approximately 20% of bids came from households holding two or more COEs. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that while prioritizing first-car households is meritorious, it is operationally difficult due to finite COE supply and potential negative impacts on car-replacing households and larger families. Consequently, the Ministry will not study giving first-car households priority access within the current COE system.

results.title: Threats against National Security Sites and Resources Expended on Investigations
results.summary: The Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) maintains multi-layered security processes, including frequent patrols, surveillance systems, and round-the-clock personnel checks, to deter and respond to threats against national security sites. A zero-tolerance stance is adopted for all credible threats, with responses ranging from evacuations to bomb sweeps, while perpetrators face stiff punishments including imprisonment. During the recent bomb hoax at Paya Lebar Air Base, the SAF executed planned responses in coordination with the Singapore Police Force (SPF) to identify and apprehend the perpetrator promptly. The specific case remains under investigation, and the SAF continues to assess threats robustly to ensure the safety of personnel, equipment, and infrastructure despite varying levels of threat credibility.

results.title: Tightening Requirements for Conversion of Foreign Driving Licences
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs stated that the Traffic Police will continue monitoring road traffic trends involving motorists converting foreign driving licences. A review of conversion requirements will be conducted where necessary based on these trends. No specific timeline, new eligibility criteria, or immediate tightening measures were announced in this response. The Minister indicated that any changes to requirements will be implemented only if monitoring data warrants further action.

results.title: Tracking Compliance with Mandated Vaccinations Against Measles and Diphtheria
results.summary: In 2024, measles vaccination coverage for children aged two and seven was 93% and 97% respectively, while diphtheria coverage for two- and 11-year-olds reached 89% and 95%. To address non-compliance, the Ministry sends HealthHub notifications and reminder letters to parents, requires proof of immunity during preschool enrolment, and offers catch-up vaccinations in primary and secondary schools. These measures maintain high coverage levels, with detailed surveillance data available in the Reports on Communicable Diseases Surveillance in Singapore.

results.title: Tracking Post-disbursement Outcomes of Productivity Solutions Grant and Enterprise Development Grant Recipients
results.summary: The Ministry tracks post-disbursement outcomes for Productivity Solutions Grant and Enterprise Development Grant recipients through regular reviews of solution effectiveness and vendor performance based on recipient feedback and project milestones. Solutions deemed less effective or vendors with poor track records are removed from pre-approved lists, while those breaching terms face investigation, suspension, or debarment from Government schemes. The Enterprise Development Grant specifically monitors approved projects to ensure committed milestones are met, with severe violations leading to enforcement actions. No specific timeline for post-first-year tracking or distinct safeguards for other grant schemes were detailed in the response.

results.title: Tracking of Jobs Relocated Overseas or Converted to Overseas Remote Arrangements
results.summary: The Ministry for Manpower does not track Singapore-based roles relocated overseas or converted to overseas-remote arrangements. Consequently, there are no annual figures available by sector or occupation for the past five years. The Ministry has stated no plans to establish such tracking, citing that transborder business reorganisation is a common global feature often involving multiple countries. It is also deemed impractical to require companies to specify exactly which role moved to which country.

results.title: Traffic Priority System and GLIDE System Synchronisation and Peak Period Public Buses Priority at Key Intersections
results.summary: The Traffic Priority System (TPS) is currently synchronised with the Green Link Determining (GLIDE) system to prioritise emergency ambulances conveying life-threatening patients near hospitals. The Government will consider extending TPS to public buses at key intersections during peak periods, pending an assessment of impacts on other road users. No specific timeline or eligibility criteria for bus inclusion have been stated. The decision to expand the system remains under review.

results.title: Update on Key Initiatives under National Mental Health and Well-being Strategy, and Assessment of Outcomes
results.summary: Since launching the National Mental Health and Well-being Strategy in late 2023, the Government has established the National Mental Health Office (NMHO) to coordinate multi-agency initiatives, including the Tiered Care Model, expanded service capacity, and training for over 160,000 personnel. The NMHO tracks population-level indicators covering outcomes, accessibility, and literacy, with performance regularly reviewed. Regarding public data publication, the Minister confirmed the Government will share meaningful data publicly over time, noting that mental health outcomes require medium- to longer-term observation. Consequently, specific metrics like self-harm rates or the treatment gap have not yet been re-measured or published, as the strategy is still in its early stages.

results.title: Wage Offsets and Other Support to Encourage Hiring of Chronically Ill Persons
results.summary: The Minister clarified that wage offsets like the Enabling Employment Credit are primarily for permanent disabilities, not chronic illnesses such as kidney dialysis. Instead, support focuses on flexible work arrangements, workplace adjustments, and case-by-case duty modifications under Tripartite Guidelines. Employers can access resources from the Singapore National Employers Federation and agencies like Workforce Singapore for job matching and career advisory services. The Government is collaborating with tripartite partners and the National Kidney Foundation to raise employer awareness and enhance employability for those with chronic conditions, addressing reported higher unemployment rates without expanding direct financial credits for this specific group.

results.title: Yearly Demographics from 2021 to 2026 on Career Conversion and Mid-Career Pathways Programme Applicants and Participants in Age Bands
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower provided appended tables detailing applicant and participant profiles for the Career Conversion Programme (CCP) and Mid-Career Pathways Programme (MCPP) across five age bands from 2021 to 2026. CCP applicant numbers exceed participant counts due to common programme dynamics and multiple applications by individuals. Conversely, the Ministry of Manpower does not track MCPP applicant numbers, limiting data availability for that specific initiative. Detailed breakdowns by age groups are available in the referenced appendices.

results.title: Yearly Demographics on Employment Departures Due to Eldercare Commitments from 2021 to 2025 and Considerations for Mandated Eldercare Leave for Singaporean Working Adults
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, an average of 1,500 residents annually left employment due to eldercare commitments, with specific demographic data provided in Table 1. The Government will not mandate eldercare leave but instead prioritizes flexible work arrangements (FWAs) as the primary support mechanism for working caregivers. This approach balances diverse caregiving needs with employers' operational requirements and the employability of caregivers. Support is delivered through the Tripartite Guidelines on FWA Requests (TG-FWAR), implementation resources, and training provided by tripartite partners and the Institute of Human Resource Professionals. The Government will continue reviewing support schemes for working caregivers.

results.title: Debate on Annual Budget Statement
results.summary: The Government supports Budget 2026 as a prudent positioning measure that treats the fiscal surplus as strategic insurance against global volatility rather than a sign of over-taxation. While acknowledging structural challenges like cost-of-living pressures and AI-driven labour disruption, the administration emphasizes that GDP growth no longer reliably translates to jobs, necessitating a national bonus anchored to creating good jobs in the age of AI. To address these issues, the Government will adopt AI-centric development as the default methodology for all new e-services, co-develop systems with world-leading firms, and evolve R&D investment vehicles into long-term strategic shareholders. Furthermore, social support mechanisms will be repositioned as a universal, progressive dividend, with low-income households receiving income-generating assets, while new revenue taps will be pursued to avoid burdening lower-income households.
